refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain a p95 end-to-end teleoperation latency of ≤ 150 ms between the Tele-operator interface and the Vehicle actuators under specified network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was qualitative; adding the p95 metric aligns with the system constraints and provides a measurable threshold for safety-critical remote operation.
refined_fr.verification_idea: End-to-end hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing with simulated network jitter and packet loss to measure timestamped command-to-actuation delay.
sub_frs: Minimize command processing latency on vehicle controller

candidate_dps: Low-latency WebRTC-based streaming and control protocol
selected_dp.selected_dp: Low-latency WebRTC-based streaming and control protocol with QoS-enabled network prioritization.

coupling_check.notes: The DP is logically decoupled from the safety logic itself, focusing purely on the transport and processing speed.

assumptions_risks: Assumes the Network Link supports Layer 3 QoS tagging.
Risk: High network congestion at the worksite may exceed the protocol's compensation capabilities.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure the DP explicitly includes hardware-accelerated encoding to meet the p95 target.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The software and hardware stack responsible for capturing operator inputs, encoding streams, and managing the remote session state.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Carries the low-latency control packets and video feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle status data required for operator feedback.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary logic for latency management resides here.
Only handles the terminal execution of commands.
Monitors for safety violations but does not manage transport latency.
Too broad; would lead to high diffusion across sub-systems.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: By centralizing the latency-sensitive protocol logic within the Teleoperation module, we minimize cross-module handshakes that introduce jitter.
containment_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module's onboard processing speed for the final 'hop' of the command.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a strict latency budget for the Vehicle module interface to ensure the Teleoperation module can meet the total 150ms goal.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The WebRTC DP is a proven industry standard for achieving sub-200ms latency in remote operations.

diffusion_risks: Network Link performance is external to the module's direct control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The logic is well-contained within the Teleoperation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; latency can be measured at the module boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership assigned to the Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Formalize the 'Network Link' interface requirements as a prerequisite for the Teleoperation module.

decision.justification: The high-level FR and DP are validated and assigned to a module. Decomposition into sub-FRs (encoding, transport, decoding) is required to manage the high safety risk and technical complexity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-TELE-002: Transmit control packets with high-priority QoS tags
FR-TELE-003: Process incoming vehicle telemetry within 20ms

assumptions: Worksite network provides at least 10Mbps uplink per vehicle.
open_risks: Hardware encoding latency on the vehicle side may vary by camera model.
evidence_needed: Vendor specifications for video encoder latency
